title: uxtextfield
html:
uxtextfield

  • Last UpdatedMai 06, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

The uxtextfield widget is a single-line text input control. It allows entry of various formats of single-line text, including plain text, text according to a certain pattern, date, tel, email, password, and URL.

Default Logical Widgets

The default logical widgets are:
  • ux-DatePicker
  • ux-DateTimePicker
  • ux-EmailField
  • ux-PasswordField
  • ux-TelField
  • ux-TextField
  • ux-TimePicker
  • ux-UrlField

Triggers

(web)trigger onchange: This trigger is fired when any of the following actions are true:
  • Value is changed (mandatory condition) and then if any one of the following action occurs:
    • clicked outside textfield (widget looses focus)
    • changebutton is enabled or present and the Enter key is pressed
    • changebutton is enabled or present and the Change or Apply button is clicked

Extends fluent-text-field Web Component

This widget is implemented using the Fluent web component fluent-text-field. Most of its features are also available in this widget. Most of its events are mapped to triggers. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-text-field web component.

Keyboard support

  • Text input
  • Horizontal navigation keys to navigate the text-inputted value
  • Vertical navigation keys move the cursor to the start or end of the text input
  • Enter key invokes the change button when enabled

Layout processing

Widget class method processLayout() constructs the template HTML for this widget. It generates nodes for the control itself as well as slots for label, prefix, suffix, changebutton, and error icon, and returns it to Uniface. Use this function to enhance the widget with more static features.

Widget properties

The uxtextfield widget supports the following properties:

25 rows found
Table 1. Widget Properties
PropertyDescription
25 rows found
25 rows found
Property
Description
html:title Defines a hover over text or tooltip, currently used as helper text.
html:hidden Defines a boolean value that hides or shows a textfield.
html:tabindex Allows or prevents a field from getting sequential focus usually with the Tab key.
html:readonly Defines the widget as being read-only.
html:placeholder Defines the placeholder text to be shown when no field value is specified.
html:disabled Defines the widget as being disabled.
html:maxlength Defines the maximum number of input characters allowed.
html:minlength Defines the minimum number of input characters allowed.
html:pattern Defines the regular expression to which an inputted value must match in order to valid.
html:size Sets the width of the element to a specified number of characters.
html:spellcheck Defines whether browser spell-checking should be applied on the inputted text.
html:type Defines the type of text input. The available options are:
  • text
  • email
  • password
  • tel
  • url
  • date
  • time
  • datetime-local
html:appearance Defines the appearance of the widget. The available options are:
  • outline (default)
  • filled
Note: It is not promoted by the Property Inspector.
class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes a specified class.
label-text Defines the label text.
Note: The label text as defined by the associated label is not supported.
label-position Defines the position of the label with respect to its widget. The available options are:
  • above(default)
  • below
  • before
  • after
Note: If the label-position is not defined for the widget, then it is aligned by default above the widget.
prefix-icon1) Defines the name of the icon displayed before the input control.
prefix-text Defines the text displayed before the input control. Ignored if prefix-icon is specified.
suffix-icon1) Defines the name of the icon displayed after the input control.
suffix-text Defines the text displayed after the input control. Ignored if suffix-icon is specified.
changebutton Defines whether the change button is shown. The available options are:
  • false (default) – not shown; only leaving the field fires the onchange trigger.
  • true – shown; clicking the changebutton or pressing the Enter key fires the onchange trigger.
changebutton:icon1) Defines the name of the icon of the changebutton.
changebutton:icon-position Defines the position of the icon of the changebutton. The available options are:

  • start - The icon is displayed before the text. The icon position flips in an RTL language. This is the default value.

  • end - If not specified or invalid and icon is specified, then icon-position will be at the end after the text. The icon position flips in an RTL language.
changebutton:value Defines the text of the changebutton.
exclude Defines that there will be no column for the field in the uxdatagrid.
Note: This is actually not a widget property. It is a field property, used by the uxdatagrid widget if that is the containing entity's widget.
1) - Icons are provided by Fluent UI Icons. Note that, if an invalid icon name is defined, no icon nor an error indication is displayed.

Helper and validation error visualization

A uxtextfield deals with the following error visualizations:
  • Red color border alert
  • Alert icon (before suffix icon)
  • Tool-tip when hovering the "Alert icon" showing the error message
  • When the uxtextfield or Change button has focus, no red border is shown, only error icon is shown
  • When the uxtextfield does not have focus, both red border and error icon is shown

UI blocking

When the field is in transaction, the uxtextfield widget blocks user input by becoming read-only. This avoids race conditions.

Selected node(s)
.u-text-field Widget node
.u-text-field.u-sw-changebutton-shown Widget node with the change-button enabled
.u-text-field.u-invalid Widget node when the value is invalid
.u-text-field.readonly or .u-text-field[readonly] Widget node when the widget is read-only
.u-text-field.disabled or .u-text-field[disabled] Widget node when the widget is disabled
.u-text-field:hover Widget node when the widget is hovered
.u-text-field:active Widget node when the widget is active
.u-text-field:focus Widget node when the widget has focus
.u-text-field:focus-within Widget node when the widget has focus-within
.u-text-field:focus-visible Widget node when the widget has focus-visible
.u-text-field::part(root) Root node
.u-text-field::part(label) Label node
.u-text-field::part(control) Control node
.u-text-field::part(start) Start node
.u-text-field::part(end) End node
.u-text-field .u-label-text Label text node
.u-text-field .u-prefix Prefix icon/text node
.u-text-field .u-suffix Suffix icon/text node
.u-text-field .u-error-icon Error icon node
.u-text-field .u-sw-changebutton Change-button button sub-widget
.u-text-field[u-label-position=<position-value>] Widget node with u-label-position=<position-value>. The available values for position-value are:
  • above
  • below
  • before
  • after
.u-stretchable Defines how the widget stretches, allowing it to resize dynamically based on the layout rules.

Widget sources

The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/text_field.

    This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-TimePicker properties.

  • ux-UrlField

    This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-UrlField properties.

  • UX Widgets

    Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder.
